overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research, publications, and job opportunities within the field of computational social science and disinformation studies. LinkedIn has the largest follower base, while engagement varies across platforms. Content consistently revolves around research findings, conference presentations, and career opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Sharing links to published research papers and articles.
Discussion and analysis of disinformation and coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms.
Announcing research positions, postdoctoral opportunities, and calls for PhD students.
Focus on computational methods for analyzing social media data.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency on Mastodon to match other platforms and improve engagement.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., videos, infographics) on all platforms to see what resonates best with the audience.
Actively participate in relevant conversations and engage with other users' content to increase visibility and expand reach.
Use relevant hashtags consistently to improve discoverability of posts.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a good balance of research announcements and conference participation updates. The repost rate is high, indicating good shareability. Focus on continuing to share research and participate in community conversations.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon engagement is relatively lower than other platforms. Consider posting more frequently and engaging with relevant communities on the platform. Focus on content that resonates with the academic and research-oriented audience on Mastodon.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn performs strongest in terms of followers and overall engagement. Leverage LinkedIn's professional network by posting about career opportunities, research grants, and professional achievements.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
